Title: Wood forming tissue-specific bicistronic expression of PdGA20ox1 and PtrMYB221 improves both the quality and quantity of woody biomass production in a hybrid poplar

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus alba × P. tremula var. glandulosa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes successive C-20 oxidations in late gibberellin biosynthesis, increasing the pool leading to bioactive GAs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stem elongation, xylem expansion, and woody biomass production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes increased stem height, stem fresh weight, stem diameter, secondary xylem formation, and overall woody biomass quantity; tissue-specific expression minimizes GA overproduction defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DX15-driven bicistronic expression including PdGA20ox1 increased biomass in Arabidopsis and hybrid poplar, with stem height up to ~2-fold in Arabidopsis and ~1.85-fold in poplar. 2. In greenhouse and field conditions, PdGA20ox1-containing transgenics showed markedly increased stem fresh weight/biomass and enhanced secondary xylem differentiation. 3. Compared with constitutive 35S::PdGA20ox1, developing-xylem-specific expression preserved biomass gains while minimizing small leaves, chlorosis, weak stems, poor rooting, and low winter survival.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic poplar, specifically expressed in stem/developing xylem under the DX15 promoter; in 35S control, expressed in stems and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; LMO field growth; promoter context (DX15 tissue-specific versus 35S constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis and hybrid poplar; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; qRT-PCR; greenhouse and field phenotyping; histology of xylem
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in GA production for biomass enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive expression caused poor rooting, reduced leaf area, pale green leaves, weak stems, and low winter survival; these were minimized by DX15-driven expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering woody biomass yield by increasing GA production specifically in wood-forming tissues; best deployed with tissue-specific promoters and in gene stacks improving biomass quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study evaluated PdGA20ox1 mainly as one component of a bicistronic cassette with PtrMYB221; protein production from the 2A system was inferred from phenotype but not directly shown.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus alba × P. tremula var. glandulosa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with C-terminal EAR-like repression motif (DLNLEL)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin biosynthesis and secondary cell wall modification
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PtrPAL1; Ptr4CL2; PtrCOMT2; PtrCCoAOMT1; PtrC4H1; PtrF5H1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses expression of multiple lignin biosynthetic genes, reducing lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secondary xylem / woody stem cell walls
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides waterproofness, durability, and mechanical strength to secondary cell walls; reduced lignin improves saccharification and bio-oil processing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces lignin content in wood, increases holocellulose/cellulose proportion, causes irregular xylem with reduced secondary wall thickening, and improves saccharification efficiency
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A decisive negative regulator used specifically to lower lignin deposition and improve biomass processing quality without compromising overall tree growth when coexpressed with PdGA20ox1 in developing xylem
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtrMYB221 was selected as a secondary wall-modifying repressor to improve biomass quality in bicistronic transgenic poplar. 2. Its expression correlated with irregular xylem and significant repression of several lignin pathway genes. 3. Transgenic poplars showed ~16 wt% lower lignin, increased holocellulose, and up to 8% higher saccharification efficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing xylem/stem under the DX15 promoter as part of the bicistronic PdGA20ox1-2A-PtrMYB221 transcript
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Bicistronic transgenic expression in hybrid poplar and Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR of lignin genes; histology; cell wall composition analysis; saccharification assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; LMO field growth; NaOH or water pretreatment during saccharification assay
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced lignin can weaken mechanical strength in principle, but no major growth fitness penalty was observed in field conditions in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable repressor for engineering lower-lignin, higher-processability woody biomass; can be stacked with growth-promoting genes under tissue-specific promoters
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen because it is the closest homolog of EgMYB1 and orthologous to other poplar repressors; direct protein-level confirmation of 2A cleavage/products was not provided.
